content.full_text:
R. ZAIDEN CORRADO
A PROFESSIONAL CORPORATION

13200 CROSSROADS PARKWAY NORTH, SUITE 345-A
CITY OF INDUSTRY, CALIFORNIA 91746-3409
(310) 908-7800

June 10, 1992


MEMORANDUM


TO:     FPPC
        428 J Street
        Suite 800
        Sacramento, CA 95814

FROM:   R. ZAIDEN CORRADO, CITY ATTORNEY

RE:     CONFLICTS INQUIRY: COUNCILMAN ERNEST SCOTT, CITY OF
        ADELANTO

        Enclosed please find inquiry from Councilman Scott and our response to same.
Mr. Scott has now through us asked for an opinion from FPPC on the matters contained
therein.

        May we have such opinion at your earliest convenience.

Very truly yours,


R. ZAIDEN CORRADO
City Attorney for
City of Adelanto

RZC/jh

DESERT GLEN REALTY
REAL ESTATE SALES AND DEVELOPMENT

14145 HIGHWAY 395 • VICTORVILLE, CALIFORNIA 92392-9338
(619) 245-5220 • FAX: (619) 245-6470


31 May 1992

MEMORANDUM TO FILE -
Copies to:  Mary L. Scarpa, Mayor, City of Adelanto
            Patricia A. Chamberlaine, City Manager
            R. Zaiden Corrado, City Attorney

Subject :   Disclosure of Possible Conflict of Interest

     Inasmuch as memorandum to files appear to be in vogue these
days, I wish to go on record as having just learned, this morning, that a
certain business relationship with Mr. Hank Ou, associated with Available
Real Estate, may appear to be misconstrued with my real estate business and
my office as Mayor Pro Tem of the City of Adelanto.

Fact     :   I have had a business relationship with Mr. Hank Ou and various
associates of his for some two years in advising and assisting
in the purchase and sale of real property in the City of
Adelanto for a commission.

     I have recently concluded the some six months negotiations for Mr.
Hank Ou and his associates, the joint venture acquisition of
160 Acres of property in the Sphere of Influence of Adelanto.
I personally participated in the partner/ownership interest of
the acquisition to the extent of a portion of my earned commission.
The value of that certain portion of earned commission was
$30,000 and it represented 5Z of the shared ownership.

     The escrow of this transaction closed February 18, 1992.

     Those associated with me in the recent political campaign for
a seat on the Adelanto City Council were verbally advised of
my business relationship with Mr. Hank Ou, although I am not
certain whether we discussed actual dollars of commission or
per cent of ownership.

     My real estate office is currently in escrow with Mr. Hank Ou
and his associates as agent only, repeat, agent only. There
is no equity interest participation in the transaction of sale
of $880,000 of property located in the City of Adelanto.

     Although I own Desert Glen Realty, and I am the Licensed Broker
of record and associate, Miss Mi Mi Song, originated the
listing of the property and originated the interest of Mr. Hank
Ou for the purchase of the property. I am aware of Miss Song's
activity in her developing business in my office and I have not
cussed with Mr. Hank Ou the current aforementioned escrow or
its status - only with Miss Song.

31 May 1992

MEMORANDUM TO FILE - cont.

The foregoing reasonably discloses to the file those salient
points that, if not made aware, be misconstrued. Any desired further
inquiry or critique on this matter is welcome.

In like manner, I am disturbed by what has been advised to me
as having occurred yesterday afternoon at a meeting in Rosemead, California
between my sales associate, Miss Mi Mi Song and Mr. Hank Ou and some of his
associates.

Miss Song was invited by Mr. Hank Ou to a meeting at a place
of his selection in Rosemead to assist him in advising certain associates of
his in the desireability of acquiring property in Adelanto. I was aware of
the contemplated meeting. Miss Song is aware of the sensitive nature of her
being a sale associate in my office in parallel with my newly elected office
of councilman and Mayor Pro Tem for the City, and we both are aware of and
follow the tenets of conflicts of interest. Miss Song's visit to yesterday's
meeting was nothing more than if they came to our office for information.

However, Miss Song advises that during her meeting with Mr.
Hank Ou she noticed taped to a wall next to a map of the City, a certain
photograph showing the election night celebration at Maverick Stadium
Conference Hall on the morning of WednesdayApril 15, 1992. Those appearing
in the picture was Mr. Hank Ou together with Ernie Scott smoking his victory
cigar along with the winners of the election and other well wishers.

Miss Song has advised me that she never mentioned my name as a
City Official to anyone during the meeting nor that I was the owner/broker
of her Company, Desert Glen Realty. She further advised that she did not hear
Mr. Hank Ou mention my name on those occasions that he spoke English but that
there were moments when he conversed in Chinese with his associate or prospects,
and that she had no way of understanding what was said.

The meeting was only informative; nothing was transacted.

As sensitive as these political conflicts are, I suggest that
the inference of displaying the election night picture showing Mr. Hank Ou
and the newly elected officials at a sales meeting of this nature is obvious.
If it is construed that the above is not obvious then it is an intrusion and
wholly without any semblance of permission from my Company or from myself or
my associate Miss Song.

Accordingly, I am immediately moving to advise Mr. Hank Ou to
cause my 5% interest in the aforementioned property comprising 160 Acres to
be liquidated and return to me the value of my earned commission on only.

I am available to discuss with those copied any areas of concern
in this matter.

Ernest A. Scott
Mayor Pro Tem

R. ZAIDEN CORRADO
A PROFESSIONAL CORPORATION

13200 CROSSROADS PARKWAY NORTH, SUITE 345-A
CITY OF INDUSTRY, CALIFORNIA 91746-3409
(310) 908-7800

June 10, 1992

MEMORANDUM

To: Ernie Scott, Councilman

From: C.R. Martin

Re: Conflicts/Request

You have asked us to report to you regarding a possible conflict of interest as outlined in your letter dated May 21, 1992, which is attached hereto requesting guidance.

Please be advised:

1. Only the FPPC is authorized to give advice concerning G.C. 87100 and conflicts of interest thereunder; although you may share the information in this Memo, it should be done only with the explicit understanding that you should not rely upon the opinions expressed, but should use this information only to obtain a formal opinion from the FPPC.

2. You state that both before and after you election to office, you operated a real estate brokers' business in Victorville more than 2 miles outside of the City of Adelanto. In connection with such business, you had an on-going relationship with an investor in Los Angeles, and for a commission or equity position, helped the investor to buy and hold land interests within Adelanto's Sphere of Influence (but not within the City itself). You want to know whether your relationship with the investor, his commission earnings, or your equity positions place you in a present or potential conflicts situation.

3. As you are well aware, the two elements of a conflict under 87100 are:

(a) an interest of some kind, i.e., land, loan, business, investment etc., and

(b) participation in the governmental process which has or will have some impact upon that interest.

Scott, Ernie
June 8, 1992
Page 2


4.      There can be little doubt that you could have such an "interest" with
        the investor, be it an on-going business relationship, investment or other
        (query re investments in a sphere of influence as to distance from
        boundary); and it is such that if the second element were to be present, a
        possible conflicts situation could arise.

5.      As to the second element (i.e. "participation"), you have been on the
        Council since mid-April, and no indication exists that he has in any way
        participated in matters which could impact such interests. "Participation"
        means voting, influencing or otherwise affecting a governmental action
        regarding such interests.

6.      Therefore, we surmise on the basis of the information which you have
        presented that there is no present conflict under 87100, but that the actual
        nature of the "interests" and location thereof be presented to FPPC
        together with a request for definition of "participation", so that you
        may be properly advised concerning potential conflicts during his term.




RZC/CRM/jh

sections.question_synthetic: Councilman Ernest Scott seeks FPPC guidance on whether his real estate business activities and financial interests related to an investor's property acquisitions in Adelanto's sphere of influence constitute a conflict of interest under California Government Code Section 87100.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is an incoming request/transmittal letter, not an FPPC response. Contains Scott's own detailed disclosure memorandum dated May 31, 1992, plus a preliminary city attorney memo (June 10) providing non-binding analysis before formal FPPC request. No formal CONCLUSION/ANSWER section from FPPC present. The city attorney advises that only FPPC can provide authoritative guidance.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: Councilman Ernest Scott seeks FPPC guidance on whether his real estate business activities and financial interests related to an investor's property acquisitions in Adelanto's sphere of influence constitute a conflict of interest under California Government Code Section 87100.

embedding.summary: This is an incoming request package forwarding Councilman Ernest Scott's conflict of interest inquiry to the FPPC. The city attorney transmits Scott's detailed memorandum disclosing business relationships with investor Hank Ou, including a $30,000 commission/5% ownership interest in a 160-acre property acquisition, and requests formal FPPC guidance on potential conflicts of interest under Government Code Section 87100.
